channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump administration places Christopher Columbus statue on White House grounds
item.description: The Trump administration placed a statue of Christopher Columbus on the White House grounds. Some people are not happy about it.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration placed a statue of Christopher Columbus on the White House grounds. Some people are not happy about it.

(Image credit: Jim Watson)
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Iran war disrupts fertilizer exports just as U.S. farmers begin to plant crops
item.description: Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike, just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.
item.content:encoded: Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike, just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris

item.title: How the runway crash and ICE agents' arrival are affecting LaGuardia
item.description: As President Trump says he's sending Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents to help with TSA screening at U.S. airports, we hear from frustrated passengers at New York CIty's LaGuardia airport.
item.content:encoded: As President Trump says he's sending Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents to help with TSA screening at U.S. airports, we hear from frustrated passengers at New York CIty's LaGuardia airport.
item.dc:creator: Steve Kastenbaum

item.title: Trump administration to pay French company $1B to drop U.S. offshore wind leases
item.description: TotalEnergies has agreed to what's essentially a refund of its leases for projects off the coasts of North Carolina and New York, and will invest the money in fossil fuel projects instead, the Department of Interior announced.
item.content:encoded:
TotalEnergies has agreed to what's essentially a refund of its leases for projects off the coasts of North Carolina and New York, and will invest the money in fossil fuel projects instead, the Department of Interior announced.

(Image credit: Thomas Padilla)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Supreme Court skeptical of laws counting mail-in ballots after election day
item.description: The Supreme Court heard arguments today in a case that tests whether states should be allowed to count ballots that are mailed on time but arrive after Election Day. The case could have broad implications.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court heard arguments today in a case that tests whether states should be allowed to count ballots that are mailed on time but arrive after Election Day. The case could have broad implications.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: A 24-karat gold Trump coin is approved for the U.S. Mint. Here's why that's unusual
item.description: The U.S. Mint is set to produce a gold coin featuring an image of President Trump. Caroline Turco, a curator at the Money Museum, says it's a sharp break with tradition that sends a message.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. Mint is set to produce a gold coin featuring an image of President Trump. Caroline Turco, a curator at the Money Museum, says it's a sharp break with tradition that sends a message.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow

item.title: Supreme Court declines to review press freedom case
item.description: At issue was the 2017 arrest in Texas of a journalist who published news stories about a border agent's public suicide and a car crash.
item.content:encoded:
At issue was the 2017 arrest in Texas of a journalist who published news stories about a border agent's public suicide and a car crash.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Kapasi

item.title: Pilot and copilot killed in collision between jet and fire truck at LaGuardia airport
item.description: A pilot and co-pilot were killed after a collision at New York's LaGuardia Airport. An Air Canada regional jet struck a fire truck on the runway after landing late Sunday night.
item.content:encoded: A pilot and co-pilot were killed after a collision at New York's LaGuardia Airport. An Air Canada regional jet struck a fire truck on the runway after landing late Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Steve Kastenbaum

item.title: As D.C.'s cherry blossom trees near peak bloom, here's a guide to their history
item.description: The renowned trees along Washington, D.C's Tidal Basin were sent as a gift from Japan in 1912. Some of the original trees are still there.
item.content:encoded:
The renowned trees along Washington, D.C's Tidal Basin were sent as a gift from Japan in 1912. Some of the original trees are still there.

(Image credit: Kayla Bartkowski)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Mapping ICE's expanding footprint, and the communities fighting back
item.description: Resistance in both Democratic and Republican cities points to broader unease with the direction of immigration enforcement.
item.content:encoded:
Resistance in both Democratic and Republican cities points to broader unease with the direction of immigration enforcement.

(Image credit: Brent Jones)
item.dc:creator: Eric Westervelt
